python3 -c "
import openpyxl
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('input.xlsx', rich_text=True)
print('Sheet names:', wb.sheetnames)
ws = wb['Sheet1']
print('Dimensions:', ws.dimensions)
print('Max row:', ws.max_row, 'Max col:', ws.max_column)

# Check column BD (col 56)
from openpyxl.utils import get_column_letter
print('Column BD index:', openpyxl.utils.column_index_from_string('BD'))

# Read first few rows of column BD
for row in range(1, min(35, ws.max_row+1)):
    cell = ws.cell(row=row, column=56)
    val = cell.value
    if val is not None:
        # Check for rich text / formatting
        font = cell.font
        print(f'Row {row}: value={repr(val)}, bold={font.bold}, color={font.color}')
" 2>&1 | head -80

import re

from openpyxl.cell.rich_text import CellRichText, TextBlock
from openpyxl.cell.text import InlineFont
from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment

# Load workbook
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('input.xlsx')
ws = wb['Sheet1']

# Sample rows (style 1) - rows 2-31 that have data
sample_texts = []
for row in range(2, 33):
    cell = ws.cell(row=row, column=56)
    if cell.value:
        sample_texts.append(str(cell.value))

# Extract all unique words/tokens from sample rows
patterns = set()
for s in sample_texts:
    tokens = re.split(r'[,\s]+', s)
    for t in tokens:
        if t:
            patterns.add(t)

print(f"Pattern count: {len(patterns)}")

# Build regex - sort by length (longest first) for greedy matching
# Use word boundaries for whole-word matching
sorted_patterns = sorted(patterns, key=len, reverse=True)
escaped = [re.escape(p) for p in sorted_patterns]
pattern_regex = re.compile(r'\b(' + '|'.join(escaped) + r')\b')

# Define fonts for rich text
red_bold_font = InlineFont(b=True, color='FFFF0000')
normal_font = InlineFont(b=True, color='FF000000')

# Process all BD cells (BD2:BD308)
for row in range(2, 309):
    cell = ws.cell(row=row, column=56)
    text = cell.value
    if not text or str(text).strip() == '':
        continue
    
    text = str(text)
    
    # Find all matches
    highlights = [False] * len(text)
    for m in pattern_regex.finditer(text):
        for i in range(m.start(), m.end()):
            highlights[i] = True
    
    # Check if any highlighting needed
    if not any(highlights):
        # No matches - keep as-is but ensure normal formatting
        continue
    
    # Build rich text runs
    runs = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(text):
        is_highlight = highlights[i]
        j = i
        while j < len(text) and highlights[j] == is_highlight:
            j += 1
        segment = text[i:j]
        if is_highlight:
            runs.append(TextBlock(red_bold_font, segment))
        else:
            runs.append(TextBlock(normal_font, segment))
        i = j
    
    # Create rich text cell
    rich_text = CellRichText(runs)
    cell.value = rich_text

# Save
wb.save('output.xlsx')
print("Done! Saved output.xlsx")
PYEOF
